# Index — gotchas
<!-- GENERATED by kb-render.sh — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->

- [Editing an applied migration is a silent no-op](./applied-migrations-are-immutable.md) — `gotcha` — Migrations are tracked by filename only (no checksum), so editing an already-applied file never re-executes on populated databases; all schema change is via new additive migrations
- [Some migrations are data backfills or repairs, not schema changes](./data-backfills-exist.md) — `gotcha` — One-off migrations exist that update or delete rows (backfilling new columns, repairing bad values, dropping constraints with cleanup); the KB schema reference documents final schema only
- [Duplicate numeric prefixes exist; order is strict filename sort](./duplicate-numeric-prefixes.md) — `gotcha` — Some numeric prefixes repeat across parallel feature strands; execution order is alphabetical filename order, so a new migration must sort after everything merged
- [Adding an enum value cannot run inside a transaction](./enum-add-requires-no-transaction.md) — `gotcha` — ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE is disallowed in a transaction block; such migrations carry a no-transaction marker the runner honors, and should hold exactly one statement
- [This KB may lag the live schema — check the last-synced marker first](./kb-may-lag-schema.md) — `gotcha` — The schema changes rapidly; the KB reflects it only up to the last-synced commit in log.md — newer migrations (including whole new tables) are absent until regeneration
- [Each migration file is atomic, but the run as a whole is not](./partial-application-on-failure.md) — `gotcha` — A failure mid-run leaves earlier files committed and recorded; the failed file is not recorded, and the next run resumes from it
- [DB credentials come from the repo's .env via the build script — never -e flags](./schema-baked-credentials.md) — `gotcha` — The build script injects database credentials from .env at container start; the house rule is to change credentials by editing .env and re-running the script, not by passing -e DB_* at docker run
- [Some reference tables are seeded by migrations, not by services](./seeded-reference-rows.md) — `gotcha` — A few migrations insert reference rows (git providers, integration providers including Slack, default agent definitions) at migration time; peers must not assume these tables start empty or re-seed them
